root_cause.text: The HTTP parser silently ignored invalid tokens in the `Transfer-Encoding` header instead of rejecting the request with an error, which could lead to request smuggling or misinterpretation of the message body.
root_cause.evidence: The patch changes `TokenList.parseTokenList` to return a boolean indicating success and updates `Http11Processor.java` to check this return value, triggering a `badRequest("http11processor.request.invalidTransferEncoding")` if invalid tokens are encountered.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The HTTP parser silently ignored invalid tokens in the `Transfer-Encoding` header instead of rejecting the request with an error, which could lead to request smuggling or misinterpretation of the message body.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch changes `TokenList.parseTokenList` to return a boolean indicating success and updates `Http11Processor.java` to check this return value, triggering a `badRequest("http11processor.request.invalidTransferEncoding")` if invalid tokens are encountered.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add validation to reject requests with malformed transfer-encoding headers.
intent_record.what: Added a condition to check the boolean return value of TokenList.parseTokenList and an else branch to trigger a badRequest if parsing fails.
intent_record.how: Ensures that malformed transfer-encoding headers are explicitly rejected rather than processed improperly.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a localized error message property for handling invalid Transfer-Encoding headers.
intent_record.what: Added a new property key and localized error message string for invalid Transfer-Encoding.
intent_record.how: Provides a specific, localized error message to be displayed or logged when an invalid Transfer-Encoding header is encountered during HTTP processing.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update parseTokenList to return a boolean indicating if the header values were parsed cleanly.
intent_record.what: Changed the return type of parseTokenList from void to boolean, renamed the 'result' parameter to 'collection', and added logic to return false if any nested parseTokenList call fails.
intent_record.how: Allows the method to propagate parsing failures (e.g., encountering non-token values) back to the caller instead of silently continuing.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update parseTokenList to return a boolean indicating whether the token list was parsed without encountering invalid values.
intent_record.what: Changed parseTokenList to return a boolean instead of void, renamed the result parameter, and added variables to track if invalid tokens were encountered.
intent_record.how: Allows the calling code to know whether the header parsing completed cleanly or if invalid, non-token values were encountered and skipped.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update token parsing logic to strictly validate and report whether all encountered tokens were valid.
intent_record.what: Modified the parsing loop to use a 'collection' instead of 'result', added boolean flags to track valid and invalid tokens, and changed the return value to reflect overall parsing validity.
intent_record.how: The method now strictly tracks the validity of parsed tokens and returns a boolean indicating if valid tokens were found without any invalid ones, improving strictness.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update doTestVary test helper invocation to include an additional boolean parameter.
intent_record.what: Added a boolean true argument to the doTestVary method call in the testAll method.
intent_record.how: Updates the test method invocation to pass an additional parameter, which alters the execution context of the test helper.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update test method call to include a new boolean parameter.
intent_record.what: Added a boolean argument to the doTestVary method call.
intent_record.how: Updates the test case to match a modified method signature or to explicitly enable a specific testing flag.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update doTestVary method calls to include a new boolean parameter.
intent_record.what: Added a boolean argument to doTestVary method calls in several test cases.
intent_record.how: Updates the test method invocations to align with a modified method signature, enabling the test to verify an additional boolean condition.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update doTestVary method call to include a new boolean parameter.
intent_record.what: Added a boolean argument 'false' to the doTestVary method call.
intent_record.how: Updates the method invocation to match the updated method signature, passing the required flag.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update doTestVary method call to match the new signature by providing the required boolean argument.
intent_record.what: Added a boolean argument (false) to the doTestVary method call.
intent_record.how: Updates the method invocation to conform to the new method signature, preventing compilation errors.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update test method call to include a new boolean parameter.
intent_record.what: Added a boolean 'false' parameter to the doTestVary method call.
intent_record.how: Updates the test method invocation to pass an additional explicit flag, aligning with a modified method signature.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update doTestVary method call to match its new signature with an additional boolean parameter.
intent_record.what: Added a 'false' boolean argument to the doTestVary method call.
intent_record.how: Updates the method invocation to conform to a modified signature in the test class.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update doTestVary method call to include a new boolean parameter.
intent_record.what: Added a boolean argument to the doTestVary method call.
intent_record.how: Updates the test case to match a modified method signature by explicitly passing false.

intent_record.intent_summary: Expand test coverage for TokenList parsing to include return value assertions, null handling, and multiple header inputs.
intent_record.what: Modified doTestVary to check the boolean return value of TokenList.parseTokenList and added new test cases for parsing multiple headers with and without nulls, as well as invalid headers.
intent_record.how: Expands the test coverage to verify that the parsing logic correctly returns true/false based on token validity and appropriately handles collections of headers including null values.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update the changelog to document a regression fix for Transfer-Encoding header validation.
intent_record.what: Added a changelog entry under the fix section.
intent_record.how: Documents a regression fix where invalid tokens in the Transfer-Encoding header are treated as an error.

grouping_result.intent: Add validation to reject requests with malformed transfer-encoding headers.
Add a localized error message property for handling invalid Transfer-Encoding headers.
Update token parsing logic to strictly validate and report whether all encountered tokens were valid.
Update test method call to include a new boolean parameter.
Update doTestVary method call to include a new boolean parameter.
Expand test coverage for TokenList parsing to include return value assertions, null handling, and multiple header inputs.
Update the changelog to document a regression fix for Transfer-Encoding header validation.

consistency_review.core_intent: Update token parsing logic to return a boolean indicating whether all tokens were parsed cleanly without invalid values.
Update doTestVary and similar test helper method calls to include a new boolean parameter.
Update doTestVary method calls to include a new boolean parameter.

relevance_review.reason: The group adds validation to reject malformed Transfer-Encoding headers, directly fixing the vulnerability where invalid tokens were silently ignored.
Adding an error message for invalid Transfer-Encoding headers directly supports fixing the vulnerability by enabling proper error reporting instead of silently ignoring the invalid tokens.
The group updates the token parsing logic to detect and report invalid tokens, directly addressing the root cause where invalid tokens in the Transfer-Encoding header were silently ignored.

The group consists entirely of updates to test helper methods (like doTestVary) to include a new boolean parameter. These changes are unrelated to the core vulnerability involving the Transfer-Encoding header parsing logic.

The changes only update the signature and calls to a test method (doTestVary) by adding a boolean parameter. This is a test refactoring/update entirely unrelated to the HTTP parser's handling of the Transfer-Encoding header.
The group expands test coverage for TokenList parsing, which directly tests the HTTP parser logic modified to address the vulnerability regarding invalid tokens in headers.
The group only updates the changelog documentation and does not contain any code changes that directly address the vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: When a field name is invalid, the parser silently skips to the next token instead of reporting an error.
When encountering a non-token, the parser silently skips it instead of rejecting the invalid transfer-encoding header.
This line parses the transfer-encoding header but assumes the parsed list is valid, without checking if the parser encountered invalid tokens.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement silently skips invalid tokens in the header instead of rejecting them, allowing malformed Transfer-Encoding headers to bypass validation.
This statement initiates the parsing of the Transfer-Encoding header but does not check whether invalid tokens were encountered and ignored.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: Refactor Vary parser to the more generic TokenList parser
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 101ac3947aacc8ded56d69f78090418fa77d8dbb
Author: Mark Thomas <markt@apache.org>
Date:   Wed Oct 16 17:38:53 2019 +0100

    Refactor Vary parser to the more generic TokenList parser

diff --git a/java/org/apache/tomcat/util/http/parser/TokenList.java b/java/org/apache/tomcat/util/http/parser/TokenList.java
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000..9ba88b0b3f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/java/org/apache/tomcat/util/http/parser/TokenList.java
@@ -0,0 +1,70 @@
+/*
+ * Licensed to the Apache Software Fou
bic_trace_step.reason: The parseTokenList function explicitly ignores invalid tokens instead of throwing an error or rejecting the request. If HttpParser.readToken returns null or if the subsequent character is not a comma or EOF, the code executes 'HttpParser.skipUntil(input, 0, ','); continue;', silently skipping the invalid token. This matches the vulnerability root cause.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Fix https://bz.apache.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=63864

Refactor parsing of the transfer-encoding request header to us
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit b3a8e01654549b0b79fc7c7684cd492cd5aafe4b
Author: Mark Thomas <markt@apache.org>
Date:   Tue Oct 29 18:42:12 2019 +0100

    Fix https://bz.apache.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=63864
    
    Refactor parsing of the transfer-encoding request header to use the
    shared parsing code and reduce duplication.

diff --git a/java/org/apache/coyote/http11/Http11Processor.java b/java/org/apache/coyote/http11/Http11Processor.java
index 0f24e18a31..30dd05adc5 100644
--- a/java/org/apache/coyote/http
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit refactors Transfer-Encoding parsing to use TokenList.parseTokenList(), which silently ignores invalid tokens instead of rejecting the request. This allows malformed Transfer-Encoding headers to bypass validation, introducing the request smuggling vulnerability.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:

Author: Mark Thomas <markt@apache.org>

diff --git a/java/org/apache/coyote/http11/Http11Processor.java b/java/org/apache/coyote/http11/Http11Processor.java
index fc7390f3b4..fc7b5a6bb9 100644
--- a/java/org/apache/coyote/http11/Http11Processor.java
+++ b/java/org/apache/coyote/http11/H
bic_trace_step.reason: The code parses the 'transfer-encoding' header into a comma-separated list and calls 'addInputFilter' for each token without validating if the token is valid or handling errors if an invalid token is found. This matches the vulnerability root cause where invalid tokens are silently ignored.

index 0fd5fae859..6c80fd07a3 100644
--- a/java/org/apache/coyote/http11/Http11Processor.java
+++ b/jav
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit merely pulls up the `prepareRequest()` method to a superclass. The removed code shows the parsing of the `Transfer-Encoding` header using a comma-separated loop and `String.trim()`, without explicitly rejecting invalid tokens (it only checks `addInputFilter()`). There is no fix for the vulnerability introduced in this refactoring commit.
